title: Calling Stored Procedures from SQL

Calling Stored Procedures from SQL

Calling Stored Procedures from SQL

DB2 does not support dynamic use of the CALL SQL statement. However, the connector allows the use of CALL in the Uniface ProcScript language and in the SQL workbench. The sql ProcScript statement is used to pass a CALL statement and parameters.

Syntax

The following syntax arrangements can be used:

sql "call Procedure", "Path"
sql "call Procedure using Parameter, Parameter, ...", "Path"
sql "call Procedure (Parameter, Parameter, ...)", "Path"

Arguments

  • Procedure—name of the stored procedure to be called.

  • Parameter—character string to be passed to the stored procedure in the SQLDA. Normally, this is passed as a DB2 VARCHAR parameter.. A question mark for parameter indicates the position of a VARCHAR(4000) return parameter whose value will be copied to the $result ProcScript variable.

    Parameters with type GRAPHIC can be used to pass UTF16 data. Parameters with type CHAR can be used to pass UTF8 data. For both of these types, $DEF_CHARSET must be UTF8. Otherwise characters that do not exist in the character set will be removed from the parameter and from $result.

  • Path—Uniface path string for the DB2 connector.

For example:

sql "call gettotal", "DB2:DBASE|USERID|PASSWORD"
sql "call getcount using smyth, staff", "$DB2"
sql "call /procs/countword ('a space')", "$DB2"
sql "call 'dataset(member)' using 'can''t', %%para, ?", "path"

Parameter Processing

DB2 stored procedures receive a SQLDA pointer when called. The connector prepares an SQLDA with a VARCHAR, GRAPHIC, or CHAR parameter (depending on the data type and type of database) for each parameter string passed in the CALL statement. Since these are VARCHARs, the sqlvar.data pointers in the SQLDA will point to a two-byte integer that is followed by the character string.

To make parameter processing easier in a stored procedure written in C, the connector adds a zero byte after the VARCHAR string. The length of the VARCHAR does not include the zero byte.

If the parameter is an unquoted string, the end of the string is delimited by a blank, or a comma, or a left parenthesis if the parameters are in a parenthesized list.

If parameters are in a quoted string, the end of the parameter string is marked by the closing quote or by the end of the SQL statement. Quotes within quoted strings can be included by doubling the quotes (for example ‘can’’t’). The connector removes one level of quote doubling before passing the string to the stored procedure.

If a parameter is stated as an unquoted question mark, the connector prepares a VARCHAR(4000) into which the stored procedure can place a return value. For a CALL statement invoked from the sql ProcScript statement, there should be only one return parameter in the parameter list. The value for that parameter will be returned to the $result variable. A CALL statement invoked by way of the SQL workbench can have more than one return parameter.

Error Conditions

The $status function is set by the connector after the CALL statement has been passed, just like it would for any other sql ProcScript statement. A negative $status indicates a syntax error in the CALL statement or a DB2 error during execution of the stored procedure.

Hitlist Limitations

The step size connector option allows you to limit the number of rows retrieved from a resultant set—the default value is 10 (or ten rows being returned), and the maximum is 2,147,483,648 rows. However, when invoking stored procedures, DB2 connector hitlist processing is not supported, with the result that:

  • The number of rows returned to the client can severely impact the memory available to Uniface on the client side.

  • An abend can occur if all of the available space has been used to hold the rows returned. TCP/IP communications performance can be impacted due to the amount of storage being sent across the network

Related Links
  • step size

    Set the number of rows the connector selects from the DBMS in each step of the Uniface stepped hitlist.

  • Support for Unicode in DB2 Connector

    The Uniface DB2 connector enables Unicode support (with some limitations). By default, Unicode support is enabled.

  • sql

    Pass a DML statement to the specified DBMS path.

  • $result

    Return the result of certain ProcScript commands and tree widget events.
